LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL HYATT'S MOTION TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 473.5 DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE THAT PROVISION IS IMPLICATED WHERE A LACK OF NOTICE LEADS TO A DEFAULT. HERE, DEFENDANT HAD NOTICE OF THE ACTION AND FILED A GENERAL DENIAL. ALTHOUGH THE NOTICE OF MOTION WAS DEFECTIVE, THE DATE OF THE HEARING WAS PROPERLY REFLECTED ON NUMEROUS PLEADINGS. DEFENDANT'S MAIN ISSUE IS WITH THE JULY 16, 2009 ORDER DEEMING THE ADMISSIONS ADMITTED. THOSE FINDINGS ARE CONCLUSIVE AND DEFENDANT'S APPLICATION FOR RELIEF FROM THAT DISCOVERY ORDER IS TOO LATE. DEFENDANT DOES NOT CITE GOOD CAUSE FOR RELIEF FROM THAT DISCOVERY ORDER. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jessica L. Chylik (863.5718 x103) for plaintiff and responding party; Yordi Echeverria (707.363.2951) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MICHAEL HYATT'S MOTION TO VACATE SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. CCP section 473.5 does not apply because that provision is implicated where a lack of notice leads to a default. Here, defendant had notice of the action and filed a general denial. Although the notice of motion was defective, the date of the hearing was properly reflected on numerous pleadings. Defendant's main issue is with the July 16, 2009 order deeming the admissions admitted. Those findings are conclusive and defendant's application for relief from that discovery order is too late. Defendant does not cite good cause for relief from that discovery order. Insufficient showing of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED

There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, as improperly noticed. Motion also denied on the merits. No basis to vacate the judgment. Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Request For Production And Inspection Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Jessica L. Chylik, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Robert N. Colen

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
Motion to compel response to request for production and inspection of documents and for sanctions, granted:
a. All objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Defendant shall serve, full, complete, and verified response to Plaintiff's request for production of documents, set one, within ten days of the date of notice of entry of this order;
c. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Jessica L. Chylik, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Robert N. Colen

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
Motion to deem request for admission admitted and for sanctions granted:
a. The matters set forth in plaintiff's request for admission, set one, are deemed admitted;
b. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
